title: Lastrea simulans
taxonomy: Lastrea simulans is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region. The classification of Lastrea simulans places it within the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in Asia. This genus is part of the larger family Athyriaceae, which consists of ferns with complex frond structures. The classification of Lastrea simulans reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species within the Athyriaceae family.
uses: Lastrea simulans is known to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis. Its extracts are also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate fever and pain. Additionally, Lastrea simulans is sometimes used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lastrea simulans for medicinal purposes.
